title: saussurea graminea subsp. ortholepis
intro: Saussurea graminea subsp. ortholepis, also known as the Tibetan saussurea, is a species of flowering plant in the family Asteraceae. This plant is traditionally used in Tibetan medicine and has been found to have anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which are believed to be due to the presence of sesquiterpene lactones and other bioactive compounds. The health benefits of Saussurea graminea subsp. ortholepis include its potential to reduce inflammation and alleviate symptoms associated with various diseases, such as arthritis and respiratory infections. In traditional medicine, the plant is often prepared into teas, infusions, and tinctures, and is also used in combination with other herbs to enhance its medicinal properties.
